text: The surgeon performs an endoscopic lung biopsy on a suspicious nodule located in the left upper lobe, obtaining tissue samples for diagnosis.
Following the biopsy, a significant amount of blood is visible within the chest cavity, indicating potential bleeding from the procedure.
The surgeon retracts the lung anteriorly to visually identify the source of bleeding within the chest cavity.
A large aortic hematoma is visible, with active bleeding observed from the descending thoracic aorta within the chest cavity.
Bleeding control is achieved through compression, followed by conversion to an anterior thoracotomy, exposing the chest cavity for further surgical intervention.
